tools.name: create_inbox
tools.description: Create a disposable email inbox that expires in 24 hours. Returns the generated email address, creation time, and expiry.

tools.name: check_inbox
tools.description: Check for messages in a disposable inbox. Returns a list of message summaries including sender, subject, and preview.

tools.name: read_message
tools.description: Read the full content of a specific email message including sender, recipients, subject, body text, and attachment info.

tools.name: download_attachment
tools.description: Download an email attachment. Returns images as base64, text files inline, and binary files as download URLs.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: check_inbox, read_message · Private data: download_attachment · Public sink: create_inbox, read_message, download_attachment.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: check_inbox, read_message · Private data: download_attachment · Public sink: create_inbox, read_message, download_attachment

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
